old_claim.note: Graphics Processing Units (GPUs) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because the broad deep-learning node is dated to the 1986 backpropagation representation-learning work, which predates the 1999 modern GPU scope.

source_shape.source_locator: Nature 1986 paper 'Learning representations by back-propagating errors' establishes the broad deep-learning/backpropagation representation-learning anchor without citing or requiring GPU hardware.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The broad deep-learning node is anchored in 1986 neural-network representation learning, before modern GPUs existed.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports the node chronology but cannot support a GPU prerequisite; keeping the edge would force a later hardware accelerator into an earlier conceptual technology.

ontology_before: The graph made broad deep learning depend directly on a 1999 GPU node despite a 1986 first-known date.
ontology_after: The graph treats GPUs as later accelerators for specific scaled deep-learning applications, not as a prerequisite for broad deep-learning neural networks.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: direct deep_learning_neural_networks -> graphics_processing_units_gpu edge removed.
would_reject_if: The node is rescoped to 2012 ImageNet-era GPU-accelerated deep learning.
A separate modern GPU-deep-learning-scaling node is added with a post-1999 date.
short_reason: A 1986 deep-learning node cannot depend on a 1999 GPU technology.
